common.binary

Class RandomAccessReader

  • java.lang.Object
    • common.binary.AbstractBinaryStream
      • common.binary.AbstractRandomAccessStream
        • common.binary.RandomAccessReader
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    BinaryInput, java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public final class RandomAccessReader
extends AbstractRandomAccessStream
implements BinaryInput
    A seekable binary reader backed by a RandomAccessFile.

    Supports random-access reading, peeking, mark/reset operations, configurable ByteOrder, and reading of primitive values from arbitrary file positions.

    This class is suitable for parsing binary file formats such as TIFF, PNG, JPEG, HEIF, RIFF, and other structured binary containers.

    Since:
    5 June 2026
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

      • Fields inherited from class common.binary.AbstractRandomAccessStream

        fpath, raf

      • Fields inherited from class common.binary.AbstractBinaryStream

        byteOrder, positionStack

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      RandomAccessReader(java.nio.file.Path fpath)
      Creates a reader using ByteOrder.BIG_ENDIAN.
      RandomAccessReader(java.nio.file.Path fpath, java.nio.ByteOrder order)
      Creates a reader using the specified byte order.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      boolean isEOF()
      Determines whether the file pointer is positioned at the end of the file.
      byte peek(long offset)
      Reads a single byte at an absolute offset without advancing the current file pointer.
      byte[] peek(long offset, int length)
      Reads a sequence of bytes at an absolute offset without moving the current file pointer.
      byte readByte()
      Reads a signed byte and advances the current position by one byte.
      byte[] readBytes(int length)
      Reads a sequence of bytes and advances the pointer by the specified length.
      double readDouble()
      Reads a 64-bit double respecting the current byte order.
      float readFloat()
      Reads a 32-bit float respecting the current byte order.
      int readInteger()
      Reads a 32-bit integer respecting the current byte order.
      long readLong()
      Reads a 64-bit long respecting the current byte order.
      short readShort()
      Reads a 16-bit short value respecting the current byte order.
      java.lang.String readString()
      Reads a null-terminated string using ISO-8859-1 encoding.
      java.lang.String readString(java.nio.charset.Charset charset)
      Reads a null-terminated (C-style) string using the specified character set.
      int readUnsignedByte()
      Reads an unsigned 8-bit value.
      long readUnsignedInteger()
      Reads an unsigned 32-bit integer.
      int readUnsignedInteger24()
      Reads an unsigned 24-bit integer respecting the current byte order.
      int readUnsignedShort()
      Reads an unsigned 16-bit value.

        close, getCurrentPosition, getPath, length, seek

      • Methods inherited from interface common.binary.BinaryInput

        close, getByteOrder, getCurrentPosition, length, mark, reset, seek, setByteOrder, skip

    • Constructor Detail

      • RandomAccessReader

        public RandomAccessReader(java.nio.file.Path fpath,
                          java.nio.ByteOrder order)
                   throws java.io.IOException
        Creates a reader using the specified byte order.
        Parameters:
        fpath - the file to read
        order - the byte order used when reading multi-byte values
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if the file cannot be opened

      • RandomAccessReader

        public RandomAccessReader(java.nio.file.Path fpath)
                   throws java.io.IOException
        Creates a reader using ByteOrder.BIG_ENDIAN.
        Parameters:
        fpath - the file to read
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if the file cannot be opened

    • Method Detail

      • readByte

        public byte readByte()
              throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a signed byte and advances the current position by one byte.
        Specified by:
        readByte in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the signed byte value
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs or the end of file is reached

      • readUnsignedByte

        public int readUnsignedByte()
                     throws java.io.IOException
        Reads an unsigned 8-bit value.
        Specified by:
        readUnsignedByte in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the value in the range 0-255
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs or the end of file is reached

      • readBytes

        public byte[] readBytes(int length)
                 throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a sequence of bytes and advances the pointer by the specified length.
        Specified by:
        readBytes in interface BinaryInput
        Parameters:
        length - the number of bytes to read
        Returns:
        a new array containing the read bytes
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if length is negative
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs or insufficient bytes remain

      • readShort

        public short readShort()
                throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 16-bit short value respecting the current byte order.
        Specified by:
        readShort in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the signed short value
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs or the end of file is reached

      • readUnsignedShort

        public int readUnsignedShort()
                      throws java.io.IOException
        Reads an unsigned 16-bit value.
        Specified by:
        readUnsignedShort in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the value in the range 0-65535
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

                throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 32-bit integer respecting the current byte order.
        Specified by:
        readInteger in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the signed integer value
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs or the end of file is reached

      • readUnsignedInteger

        public long readUnsignedInteger()
                         throws java.io.IOException
        Reads an unsigned 32-bit integer.
        Specified by:
        readUnsignedInteger in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the value in the range 0-4294967295
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

      • readUnsignedInteger24

        public int readUnsignedInteger24()
                          throws java.io.IOException
        Reads an unsigned 24-bit integer respecting the current byte order.
        Specified by:
        readUnsignedInteger24 in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the value in the range 0-16777215
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

        public long readLong()
              throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 64-bit long respecting the current byte order.
        Specified by:
        readLong in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the signed long value
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs or the end of file is reached

        public float readFloat()
                throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 32-bit float respecting the current byte order.
        Specified by:
        readFloat in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the float value
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

        public double readDouble()
                  throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a 64-bit double respecting the current byte order.
        Specified by:
        readDouble in interface BinaryInput
        Returns:
        the double value
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

      • peek

        public byte peek(long offset)
          throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a single byte at an absolute offset without advancing the current file pointer.
        Specified by:
        peek in interface BinaryInput
        Parameters:
        offset - the absolute position to read from
        Returns:
        the signed byte value
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the resulting position is out of file bounds
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

      • peek

        public byte[] peek(long offset,
                   int length)
            throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a sequence of bytes at an absolute offset without moving the current file pointer.
        Specified by:
        peek in interface BinaryInput
        Parameters:
        offset - the absolute position to start reading from
        length - the number of bytes to read
        Returns:
        a new byte array containing the requested data
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the requested range extends beyond the file bounds
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if either offset or length is negative
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

      • readString

        public java.lang.String readString()
                            throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a null-terminated string using ISO-8859-1 encoding.
        Specified by:
        readString in interface BinaryInput
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException
        See Also:
        readString(Charset)

      • readString

        public java.lang.String readString(java.nio.charset.Charset charset)
                            throws java.io.IOException
        Reads a null-terminated (C-style) string using the specified character set.

        If the current position already points to a null terminator, an empty string is returned and the file pointer advances by one byte. The string bytes are decoded using the supplied charset and the current position is advanced past both the string data and the null terminator.

        Specified by:
        readString in interface BinaryInput
        Parameters:
        charset - the character set used to decode the string bytes
        Returns:
        the decoded string, excluding the null terminator
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if charset is null
        java.io.IOException - if there is an I/O error
        java.io.EOFException - if the end of the file is reached without finding a null terminator

      • isEOF

        public boolean isEOF()
        Determines whether the file pointer is positioned at the end of the file.
        Returns:
        true if no bytes remain to be read, otherwise false
